claims.claimText: Orthopaedic complications form a reusable failure-mode backbone that links prevention, early recognition, workup, management, and salvage across specialties.

metadata.sourceNote: Complications manufacture scope; incidence/threshold claims require provenance and human review

claims.claimText: Effective complication care starts with risk-factor recognition and prevention rather than reactive management alone.

metadata.sourceNote: Complications manufacture scope; incidence/threshold claims require provenance and human review

claims.claimText: Infection prevention combines host optimization, soft-tissue protection, timely antibiotics when indicated, and sterile technique.

metadata.sourceNote: Complications manufacture scope; incidence/threshold claims require provenance and human review

claims.claimText: Suspected surgical site or implant infection requires structured workup before escalating to reoperation or revision.

metadata.sourceNote: Complications manufacture scope; incidence/threshold claims require provenance and human review

claims.claimText: Open fractures carry elevated risk of fracture-related infection and require early antibiotics, soft-tissue care, and debridement principles.

metadata.sourceNote: Complications manufacture scope; incidence/threshold claims require provenance and human review

claims.claimText: Nonunion evaluation addresses mechanical stability, biology, infection, host factors, and time course before revision fixation.

metadata.sourceNote: Complications manufacture scope; incidence/threshold claims require provenance and human review

claims.claimText: Delayed union and nonunion are related but not interchangeable; progression timeline and intervention threshold matter.

metadata.sourceNote: Complications manufacture scope; incidence/threshold claims require provenance and human review

claims.claimText: Loss of fixation and hardware failure often reflect construct stability mismatch, bone quality, reduction quality, or load-sharing failure.

metadata.sourceNote: Complications manufacture scope; incidence/threshold claims require provenance and human review

claims.claimText: Preserved distal pulses do not exclude acute compartment syndrome; pain out of proportion and passive stretch pain remain central.

metadata.sourceNote: Complications manufacture scope; incidence/threshold claims require provenance and human review

claims.claimText: Venous thromboembolism risk after major orthopaedic injury or arthroplasty is mitigated by risk-stratified prophylaxis and early mobilization when safe.

metadata.sourceNote: Complications manufacture; incidence estimates require source provenance before publication

claims.claimText: Deep vein thrombosis and pulmonary embolism are linked VTE events with distinct diagnostic urgency and treatment thresholds.

metadata.sourceNote: Complications manufacture scope; incidence/threshold claims require provenance and human review

claims.claimText: Prosthetic joint infection management depends on timing, organism, implant stability, and host status; options range from debridement to staged revision.

metadata.sourceNote: Management-changing claim; attending review required

claims.claimText: Aseptic loosening and periprosthetic infection can present similarly; infection must be excluded before revision for loosening.

metadata.sourceNote: Complications manufacture scope; incidence/threshold claims require provenance and human review

claims.claimText: Polyethylene wear debris can drive osteolysis and secondary implant loosening even when the patient is relatively asymptomatic.

metadata.sourceNote: Complications manufacture scope; incidence/threshold claims require provenance and human review

claims.claimText: Avascular necrosis risk tracks vascular disruption of the femoral head, talus, or humeral head after injury or treatment.

metadata.sourceNote: Complications manufacture scope; incidence/threshold claims require provenance and human review

claims.claimText: Arthrofibrosis and joint stiffness require early recognition of motion loss and coordinated therapy before advanced salvage.

metadata.sourceNote: Complications manufacture scope; incidence/threshold claims require provenance and human review

claims.claimText: Graft failure and re-tear after soft-tissue reconstruction may reflect technical, biologic, rehabilitation, or re-injury factors.

metadata.sourceNote: Complications manufacture scope; incidence/threshold claims require provenance and human review

claims.claimText: New or progressive neurologic deficit after spine injury or surgery is time-sensitive and requires immediate evaluation.

metadata.sourceNote: Complications manufacture scope; incidence/threshold claims require provenance and human review

claims.claimText: Durotomy management prioritizes watertight repair principles, posture/activity guidance, and monitoring for CSF leak sequelae.

metadata.sourceNote: Complications manufacture scope; incidence/threshold claims require provenance and human review

claims.claimText: Physeal bar formation can produce growth arrest, angular deformity, and leg-length discrepancy in skeletally immature patients.

metadata.sourceNote: Complications manufacture scope; incidence/threshold claims require provenance and human review

claims.claimText: Complex regional pain syndrome is a diagnosis of disproportionate regional pain with sensory, vasomotor, sudomotor, or motor/trophic features after injury or surgery.

metadata.sourceNote: Complications manufacture scope; incidence/threshold claims require provenance and human review

claims.claimText: Counsel that reoperation and revision surgery are management-changing endpoints requiring diagnosis of the failure mode before salvage selection.

metadata.sourceNote: Complications manufacture scope; incidence/threshold claims require provenance and human review

claims.claimText: Hospital readmission after orthopaedic care often clusters around infection, VTE, wound problems, pain control failure, and medical decompensation.

metadata.sourceNote: Complications manufacture scope; incidence/threshold claims require provenance and human review

claims.claimText: Early recognition of complications depends on serial examination, expected recovery trajectories, and explicit escalation thresholds.

metadata.sourceNote: Complications manufacture scope; incidence/threshold claims require provenance and human review

decisionPoints.trigger: Wound erythema, drainage, fever, or rising inflammatory markers after orthopaedic surgery
decisionPoints.action: Initiate infection workup, hold nonessential antibiotics when cultures are planned, and escalate for possible operative debridement

decisionPoints.trigger: Suspected infection after fracture fixation with wound issues, nonunion, or systemic signs
decisionPoints.action: Obtain targeted workup and plan debridement with implant retention versus removal based on stability and timing

decisionPoints.trigger: Clinical findings concerning for evolving acute compartment syndrome
decisionPoints.action: Remove constriction, perform serial examination, and escalate for emergent fasciotomy without delaying for imaging alone

decisionPoints.trigger: Acute dyspnea, hypoxia, chest pain, or hemodynamic change with VTE risk
decisionPoints.action: Activate urgent medical evaluation and diagnostic pathway for pulmonary embolism while supporting oxygenation and circulation

decisionPoints.trigger: New or progressive motor, sensory, or bowel/bladder deficit after spine injury or surgery
decisionPoints.action: Perform immediate neurologic examination, protect the spine as indicated, and escalate for urgent imaging and specialist intervention

decisionPoints.trigger: Loss of pulse, expanding hematoma, progressive neurologic deficit, or threatened limb perfusion
decisionPoints.action: Document status, reduce gross deformity when appropriate, and escalate immediately for vascular or nerve evaluation

decisionPoints.trigger: Suspected prosthetic joint infection with pain, effusion, wound drainage, or abnormal labs
decisionPoints.action: Complete infection workup before revision and select DAIR, one-stage, or two-stage strategy with attending input

decisionPoints.trigger: Progressive pain, radiographic lucency, or implant migration after infection is excluded
decisionPoints.action: Plan revision arthroplasty strategy based on bone loss, implant track record, and host factors

decisionPoints.trigger: Broken implant, loss of alignment, or construct failure on surveillance imaging
decisionPoints.action: Protect the limb, obtain comparison imaging, and plan revision fixation or alternative construct

decisionPoints.trigger: Failure of expected clinical or radiographic progression toward union
decisionPoints.action: Evaluate infection, stability, biology, and host factors before selecting revision or biologic augmentation

decisionPoints.trigger: Healed fracture with functionally significant malalignment, shortening, or rotation
decisionPoints.action: Quantify deformity and discuss osteotomy, revision, or accept-and-adapt pathways with attending

decisionPoints.trigger: Suspected AVN after femoral neck, talar, or proximal humerus injury
decisionPoints.action: Obtain dedicated imaging and counsel joint-preservation versus arthroplasty options by stage and joint

decisionPoints.trigger: Early progressive motion loss after fracture care or soft-tissue reconstruction
decisionPoints.action: Intensify supervised therapy, exclude infection or mechanical block, and escalate for lysis/MUA when indicated

decisionPoints.trigger: Recurrent instability or imaging evidence of graft failure after ligament reconstruction
decisionPoints.action: Assess tunnel position, graft integrity, alignment, and rehabilitation before revision reconstruction planning

decisionPoints.trigger: Asymmetric growth, angular deformity, or LLD after physeal injury
decisionPoints.action: Image the physis, quantify bar and remaining growth, and plan bar resection versus guided growth/osteotomy

decisionPoints.trigger: Chest pain, arrhythmia, hypotension, or suspected ACS after orthopaedic surgery
decisionPoints.action: Activate medical emergency response and stabilize while pausing nonessential orthopaedic progression

decisionPoints.trigger: Life-threatening decompensation or expected high mortality trajectory after major orthopaedic injury/surgery
decisionPoints.action: Escalate critical care, clarify goals of care, and coordinate multidisciplinary decision-making

decisionPoints.trigger: Early post-discharge wound, VTE, infection, or medical warning signs
decisionPoints.action: Expedite evaluation, risk-stratify for ED/readmission, and address root cause before discharge planning
